message.content:
**TikTok Script:**

**[0:00 - 0:10]**
[Intro music starts; the video opens with a scrolling list of popular hashtags and people scrolling through their mobile phone, animated around the frame. The last line is "18 keens, keens—at last."]
[Gen Z-friendly teen voice-up speaking over-the-visuals: "Hello, Gen Z, turning 18, getting a driver's license…nope, still not allowed to vote! #18keen #notvoting #why]

**[0:10 - 0:35]**
[Visual: Teen holding up a “No Voting Yet” meme, laughing, then turning serious. Quick fade into a split-screen, showing a senior activist making a passionate speech in the background.]

[Voice-up: "The seniors, the ones who cut you off on the way to the coffee shop, they’re keens to vote. They’ve been fighting for their rights since they were in our..."?

**[0:35 - 0:50]**
[Visual: Shows a flashback to the 60s-70s, adults protesting for their rights, then fast-forwards to 2021, seniors still happily voting.]

[Voice-up: "Our parents, our grandparents, and now us? Why are they fighting for their right but turning their back on us?"

**[0:50 - 1:05]**
[Visual: A timeline graphic, showing years when people were allowed to vote, but missing 18-year-olds.]

[Voice-up: "Man, 18 is not just keens, keens—at last; it’s when we’re finally adults, and we can help change the game for better or worse, right? #adultproblems #timeisticavaluable"]

**[1:05 - 1:20]**
[Visual: A split-screen between a senior activist holding a sign, and a teen sitting at a desk surrounded by memes, complaining about school]

[Voice-up: "But here’s the thing, if 18 is when we finally become adults... why can’t we have a say in our future? heck, they let us buy beer for when we graduate!"

**[1:20 - 1:35]**
[Visual: Montage of 18-year-olds doing adult tasks like working jobs, paying bills, driving cars, and studying. Then shows adults mocking their attempts to do these things. A turnaround to 18-year-olds in the same situations, now satisfied.]

[Voice-up: "We’re not just adults in training; when we were 16, 17, and 18, we were doing all the things they expect from grown-ups. So, why not vote? #adulting #readytovote"]

**[1:35 - 1:50]**
[Visual: Animated countdown from 25 to 18, heartwarming but turning to heckling and ignoring adults mocking the youth.]

[Voice-up: "Look, they heckle and ignore us... nope, we’ll stand in the gap. Vote heck or not, but we should have the right to!"

**[1:50 - 2:05]**
[Visual: Young activist in a leather jacket and hat, waving a "VOTE AT 18" sign, while hecklers and older adults in the background are now talking positively. A turnaround to the young activist’s face, looking determined.]

[Voice-up: "So, 2023 or sooner... vote at 18! Let’s not keens hecked and ignored, let’s be the change. #VOTE18"]

**[2:05 - 2:10]**
[Visual: End scene with the young activist giving a thumbs-up to the camera, then turning to the screen, looking directly at the viewer.]

[Voice-up: "Let's vote at 18! #activism #GenZStrong #timeisnow"]

**[2:10 - 2:30]**
[Outro music starts; quick cuts of youth activists, schools, and community events, then the video ends on a close-up of the young activist talking directly to the camera.]

[Voice-up: "Ready to turn keens—at last? Let’s do this! #18keens #voteage #youthpower"]

**[Final Note: Tag and hashtag for sharing the video to encourage others to support the movement. Use #Vote18 and #GenZStrong]**

This script uses Gen Z culture references and humor to appeal to their sense of fairness and frustration. Encouraging them to see lowering the voting age as a bold step to ensure their
